comments.body:
Hello Alexandra,

Apologies for the inconvenience.

I would like to inform you that our team is actively working on this case, and a fix will be included in an upcoming release.

In the meantime, whenever you make changes to products in **Shopify**, you will need to use the **Force Import All** option. This option will fetch all the latest product changes with a single click and ensure the updates start working in the app.

We appreciate your understanding and patience.

Please feel free to reach out to us anytime if you need any assistance from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

 ![Screenshot at Mar 03 11-35-21.png](https://pluginhive.zendesk.com/attachments/token/DlQfhjkeNy1LpN3a9zSggEOte/?name=Screenshot+at+Mar+03+11-35-21.png&lotus_request=true)

comments.body:
Hi Manohar,



Our web developers have tested this issue repeatedly now, and determined that your app is going dormant. Your servers are lagging or not responding during random intervals. Sometimes the rates will fail to populate, but then 30 seconds to 2 minutes later, or when we refresh the product import, it works.



This is an issue on **your end.**

This same issue has been occurring repeatedly for months now. We cannot have it keep happening.

Customers are frustrated, our customer service team is frustrated, and your cheap infrastructure server issue is costing us business.



This is your final warning. If this issue is not resolved, we will be pursuing other shipping solutions.

Please advise.



-A





Alexandra Ketterling

Brand Marketing & E-commerce Director

comments.body:
Hello Alexandra,

I understand your concern.

As mentioned earlier, the app automatically syncs products from Shopify, and this functionality is working as expected for all customers. At the moment, this is occurring only with your store.

To help us better understand why the automatic sync may not be working in this case, it would be helpful if you could share a bit more information. Specifically, could you please let us know why frequent changes are being made to the products and why each product has many variants?

This information may help our technical team review the setup more effectively and attempt to recreate the scenario from our end.

Once again, we sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and completely understand the situation.

Looking forward to hearing back from you.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Manohar,



Okay.

However, please understand that it is difficult for us, in our time zone, as a business to wait for your response when customers get frustrated at not being able to place an order.

From our end, there have been plenty of opportunities for your team to review the logs.

We can't have issues and then wait for you to export a fresh product file if required. Our products are the same products that have always been there.

I have also shared draft orders and screenshots in the past.

I need assistance from the L3 team regarding the following case.

Case: Shipping rates are failing randomly even though there have been no changes to the products.

Details:
This is a $19 MCSL app customer who has reported shipping rate failures multiple times. When the customer reports the issue and we check the logs, the logs appear blank.
Previously, we resolved the issue by force importing the products, after which the rates started displaying again.

Recently, we enabled the “Force Import All” toggle feature, so when the merchant notices the issue, they immediately perform a force import, which resolves the problem. However, by the time they realize the rates have failed, some customers have already abandoned checkout, resulting in lost sales.

The customer confirmed that no changes have been made to the products, and the same products have been used since the beginning. They are asking why a force import is required repeatedly if there are no product changes.

The customer mentioned that they have 4 products with 100+ variants.


- **Oxymesh Crewneck Tee (AJ801D)** – 189 variants
- **Oxymesh V-neck Tech Tee (AJ202D)** – 120 variants
- **DriMax™ Crewneck Expert Tee (AI801D)** – 118 variants
- **Oxymesh™ Long Sleeve Tech Tee (AJ301D)** – 105 variants

Currently, the customer is not willing to report the issue anymore or wait for us to collect logs, as they are concerned about losing sales. Instead, they immediately perform a force import whenever the issue occurs.

The customer would like to know:

- What is causing this issue?
- Why is a force import required for the app to display rates when there are no product changes?
- Is there a permanent fix for this problem?

At the moment, we do not have a rate log ID or order example where the rates failed. However, the customer is unhappy and considering uninstalling the app. Therefore, I would like to review this case with you.

Could you please share your input on how we should proceed?

Store Details:
Store URL – expertbrand-apparel.myshopify.com
Account ID – dde262f2-9a7a-4b8e-b088-b68afe8d6439
Active From – May 2022
Plan – $19 Starter plan

Hello Alexandra,

Thank you for your valuable time on the call. It was nice speaking with you.

As discussed, I made the following two changes in the app, as they might have been causing the rate calculation failure or delay:

We will continue working on this and keep you updated.
